data.title: Semtech likely to report higher Q2 earnings, expected at 40 cents per share and revenue of $256.06 million, up from 11 cents and $215.35 million last year. Stocks rose 1.9% to $49.39.
data.description: Semtech Corporation (NASDAQ: SMTC) will announce its second-quarter financial results after markets close on Monday, August 25. Analysts are anticipating earnings of 40 cents per share, a significant increase from the 11 cents per share reported in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is projected to be $256.06 million, showing growth from $215.35 million in the previous year. In the first quarter, Semtech exceeded expectations in its financial results. The company's stock price closed at $49.39 on Thursday, representing a 1.9% increase. For more details, readers can find the complete story on Benzinga.com and keep track of the latest analyst ratings in the Analyst Stock Ratings section. The ratings can be sorted by various criteria such as stock ticker, company name, analyst firm, or rating changes.

data.title: MLB in talks with NBCUniversal, Netflix, ESPN for new rights deals. NBCU nearing $200M/yr agreement. More revenue expected.
data.description: Advanced negotiations are currently underway between Major League Baseball and multiple media companies, including NBCUniversal, Netflix, and ESPN, owned by The Walt Disney Company, regarding new rights deals that may revolutionize the way fans consume the sport. Sources familiar with the discussions revealed that these deals have the potential to generate significantly higher revenue for MLB compared to its current agreement with ESPN. NBCUniversal is close to finalizing a three-year deal valued at around $200 million annually to broadcast games on both its network and streaming platform, Peacock. As per the reported terms, NBC would air Sunday night games when other prominent sports are not scheduled, while additional games would be available for streaming on Peacock. This agreement would also grant NBCU rights to certain playoff games, complementing its existing sports content.

data.title: Market reaches highs as Fed chief Powell may signal rate cuts. Tesla, Micron, and AppLovin seek new buy points. Dow Jones Futures.
data.description: Investors are optimistic as the stock market reaches record highs, while Federal Reserve Chairman Powell is expected to deliver a significant address. Tesla, Micron, and AppLovin are all showing strong momentum and establishing new buying opportunities. The Dow Jones futures indicate a possibility of rate cuts based on Powell's speech. This news has been reported without disclosing the original source.

data.title: Pharma stocks rise as Trump admin agrees to 15% tariffs on European drug imports, easing tensions.
data.description: Pharmaceutical companies experienced a sense of relief as the Trump administration authorized a 15% tariff on drug imports from Europe. This move has led to pharmaceutical stocks feeling more at ease. This development is seen as a positive for the industry as it will affect the cost of imported drugs and potentially impact investors.

Investors are eager to see if Semtech will exceed expectations and provide positive guidance for the next quarter. Past performance shows that in the last quarter, Semtech beat EPS estimates by $0.01, resulting in a 4.56% drop in share price. Analysts have given Semtech a consensus Buy rating with an average one-year price target of $50.67, indicating a potential 2.59% upside. Comparing ratings and price targets with peers like Silicon Laboratories, Rigetti Computing, and Impinj can give further insights for investors. Market reactions are often driven by guidance rather than just earnings performance.

data.title: Dow surges over 300 points, reaching 45,109.99. BJ's Wholesale shares fall 7% after Q2 results; NASDAQ up 0.37% at 21,177.85.
data.description: This morning, U.S. stocks saw a rise, with the Dow Jones index surging over 300 points on Friday. The Dow climbed 0.72% to 45,109.99, while the NASDAQ increased by 0.37% to 21,177.85. The S&P 500 also experienced a rise, gaining 0.53% to 6,404.23. Communication services sector shares surged by 0.9%, and information technology stocks rose by 0.2%. BJ's Wholesale Club Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: BJ) shares fell approximately 7% after reporting a revenue miss in the second quarter. The company posted second-quarter adjusted earnings per share of $1.14, surpassing the analyst estimate of $1.09. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: 1 year post Durov's arrest: CEO required in France during investigation. What's next after 365 days?
data.description: Pavel Durov, the CEO of Telegram, was arrested approximately one year ago and has been mandated to remain in France as part of an ongoing investigation.

data.title: Coinbase predicts 300% surge in stablecoin market cap to $1.2 trillion by 2028, based on US Treasuries model.
data.description: A US-based cryptocurrency exchange, Coinbase, is projecting a substantial increase in the stablecoin market cap, potentially reaching $1.2 trillion by 2028, marking a 300% surge from the current level. This forecast is informed by a model considering variables like the volume of US Treasuries issued, given that stablecoins are commonly backed by US dollars or Treasuries. According to Coinbase's analysis, this growth doesn't require significant or sustained rate disruptions but relies on gradual, policy-driven adoption accumulating over time. The exchange emphasizes that the market for dollar-pegged crypto assets is at a critical stage, with the potential for accelerated adoption.

data.title: El Salvador sees hundreds graduate with Bitcoin diplomas, 350 female students complete course. Initiative led by National Bitcoin Office.
data.description: In El Salvador, 350 female students graduated from the Bitcoin Diploma course at Instituto Nacional General Francisco Morazán. The initiative is part of the country's efforts to educate citizens on Bitcoin. The initiative is run by the National Bitcoin Office and has been extended to other institutions in the country. The Bitcoin Diploma was created by Mi Primer Bitcoin and provides free education materials covering the history of money, fiat currencies, and Bitcoin. The country's education minister, Karla Trigueros, attended the graduation ceremony. This reflects El Salvador's broader push to ensure its citizens understand the leading cryptocurrency. Initiatives like this aim to further integrate Bitcoin into the country's economy and daily life.

data.title: Global liquid biopsy market valued at USD 3.65B in 2024, USD 4.03B in 2025, projected to reach USD 7.05B by 2030 with 11.8% CAGR. Major drivers include rising cancer incidence, precision medicine adoption, and technological advancements. Liquid biopsy plays key role in early disease detection, oncology care, and personalized medicine. Expanding beyond oncology into prenatal testing and organ transplantation monitoring. Innovations like NGS and digital PCR enhancing market growth.
data.description: The global liquid biopsy market is experiencing significant growth, with a value of US$4.03 billion in 2025 and projected to reach US$7.05 billion by 2030, at a CAGR of 11.8%. Factors driving this surge include increasing cancer incidence, adoption of precision medicine, and technological advancements. Liquid biopsy offers a minimally invasive alternative to tissue biopsies, allowing for the detection of circulating tumor DNA and other biomarkers through a blood test. This provides real-time insights for timely treatment decisions, especially important as cancer cases climb globally. The technology is expanding beyond oncology into areas like prenatal testing and organ transplantation monitoring. Innovations like next-generation sequencing and digital PCR are enhancing the sensitivity and clinical utility of liquid biopsy assays, making it an essential tool in healthcare.

data.title: Inspira Technologies OXY B.H.N. Ltd. gains U.S. Patent for ART500 core tech, securing lead in $20B market for respiratory support.
data.description: Inspira™ Technologies OXY B.H.N. Ltd., based in Ra'anana, Israel, a leading company in life-support and diagnostic technologies, has recently obtained a U.S. Patent for a low flow rates extracorporeal oxygenation system and its methods of application. This patent is crucial as it covers the foundational technology of the ART500 tool, which is vital in Inspira's plan to enter the advanced respiratory support market estimated at $20 billion. The patent includes 16 unique claims, solidifying the innovative nature of the technology.

data.title: Workday's stock drops as revenue guidance disappoints despite exceeding earnings expectations in second quarter.
data.description: The stock of Workday experienced a decline following the company's announcement of second-quarter earnings that exceeded expectations. However, the guidance provided by the software maker was in line with analyst forecasts. This news led to a drop in Workday's stock value.

data.title: Nvidia halts H20 production post Beijing directive to stop AI chip purchase in China. NVDA stocks indicated slightly lower.
data.description: According to reports, Nvidia has ceased production of the H20 chip after receiving a directive from Beijing advising local companies against purchasing the AI chip. This decision comes just weeks after the Chinese government's recommendation. As a result of this news, NVDA stock was slightly down.
